title: Baccharis libertadensis
taxonomy: Baccharis libertadensis is classified under the family Asteraceae. This family, also known as the daisy or sunflower family, comprises approximately 23,000 species. Baccharis libertadensis is further classified under the genus Baccharis, which includes around 300 species of shrubs and small trees. The genus Baccharis is native to the Americas, and the species are widely distributed in South America. Baccharis libertadensis is specifically found in the Andean region of South America.
uses: Baccharis libertadensis, a plant native to the Andes mountains, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. Its leaves and stems contain flavonoids and polyphenols, which are believed to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Additionally, Baccharis libertadensis has been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Baccharis libertadensis as a medicinal treatment.
